_readme: Drinking-water Maximum Contaminant Levels (MCLs) for the DIE nm_mcl_exceedance product. This file is the source of truth; upload it to gs://<products_bucket>/config/mcl.json. Each top-level key (except those starting with '_') is a DIE analyte name; the product compares each well's latest value to that analyte's 'mcl' and flags value > mcl. Analytes absent here are reported without a flag.

_schema.mcl: number — the threshold, in 'units'. Used by the product (value > mcl => exceedance).
_schema.units: string — units of mcl; MUST match DIE's normalized output units for the analyte (mg/L).
_schema.type: string — 'primary' (enforceable health-based NPDWR) or 'secondary' (non-mandatory aesthetic SMCL). Used by the product.
_schema.basis: string — what the value is measured as (e.g. nitrate 'as N'); the data MUST be on the same basis for the comparison to be valid.
_schema.label: string — human-readable contaminant name.

_source.primary: EPA National Primary Drinking Water Regulations — https://www.epa.gov/ground-water-and-drinking-water/national-primary-drinking-water-regulations

nitrate.label: Nitrate (as nitrogen)
nitrate.note: EPA MCL is 10 mg/L measured as nitrogen (N). If DIE reports nitrate as NO3, convert (as-NO3 MCL ~= 44.3 mg/L) or this flag is wrong.

fluoride.label: Fluoride
fluoride.note: Primary (health) MCL 4.0 mg/L. A secondary aesthetic SMCL of 2.0 mg/L also exists; this file uses the enforceable primary MCL.

_omitted.ph: EPA secondary standard is a RANGE (6.5-8.5), not a single MCL, so it does not fit the value > mcl test and is excluded.
